返回日报
Hacker News #9

Kimi 工作

Kimi Work

AI 作者 ms7892 热度 628 回复 257 2026/07/21 01:13
打开原帖 打开外部链接 JSON

摘要

  • The AI Desktop for Knowledge WorkYour intelligent local agentDeeply connected to your local files. Capable of browser automation. Running around the clock. Built for maximum productivitySet It & Forget It: 24/7 AutomationYour workflow never sleeps. Powered by a robust built-in Cron engine, Kimi Work automates your repetitive tasks. Whether it's an early-morning LLM Agent call to draft daily briefings, or a midnight Python script to process massive datasets, Kimi runs quietly in the background, exactly on time.WebBridge: Your Autonomous Web AgentGive Kimi a goal, and watch it navigate the internet like a human. Powered by WebBridge, it autonomously browses across tabs, extracts critical data, and executes multi-step web tasks. You provide the prompt, Kimi handles the clicks, scrolls, and research.Try WebBridgeAgent Swarm & Instant Office CreationTackle complex problems with the power of Swarm Intelligence. Kimi automatically coordinates multiple specialized agents to break down and solve multi-layered tasks simultaneously. Once the research is complete, seamlessly convert insights into professional PowerPoint decks or Excel sheets in seconds.Built for Finance: Native Global Market DataMeet your desktop chief analyst. Kimi Work comes pre-integrated with deep data sources for A-shares, HK stocks, and US equities. Skip the complex API setups - instantly pull earnings reports, analyze market anomalies, and reconcile spreadsheets through natural conversation. Gain a crucial edge in a fast-paced market.Get Started with Kimi WorkThink, create, and execute, all from your desktop. Kimi Work brings intelligence to every step of your workflowFAQWhile the Kimi web app is perfect for quick chat and queries, Kimi Work is a Local Agent designed for deep workflows. It mounts your local folders, navigates the web autonomously via WebBridge, runs Python code in the background, and executes scheduled tasks. It's a system-level digital employee.You have absolute control over your files. The built-in Ask before acting safeguard means Kimi will prompt you for explicit authorization before it modifies, overwrites, or runs code within your local directories. Nothing happens without your consent.WebBridge gives Kimi the ability to use a browser like a human. You can tell it to check the latest news on a website and summarize it, or scrape historical stock data to your local Excel. It clicks, scrolls, and extracts data autonomously, saving you hours of manual work.Our Cron scheduler supports LLM Agent Calls, Python/Shell executions, and more. You can trigger tasks daily, hourly, or conditionally. To ensure tasks run seamlessly overnight, simply toggle the Keep Computer Awake option in your settings.

命中 3 个 AI/科技关键词。

正文翻译

暂无正文

暂无原文

回复翻译 20

wxw 2026/07/21 04:18
> Kimi Work 是专为深度工作流程设计的本地代理。它会挂载您的本地文件夹，通过 WebBridge 自主导航网络，在后台运行 Python 代码，并执行计划任务。

这显然是 Claude/Codex 产品的骗局（尤其是 Codex，在样式方面），但我认为 Kimi 的目标只是与更大的实验室在功能上保持一致。我怀疑他们是否愿意投入大量资金从头开始为“代理工作的未来”设计一个用户界面。

他们的面包和黄油和声望将继续是低成本、接近前沿的模型，为此他们至少需要有一些应用程序层可以插入。

而且，在某些方面，欺骗是向人们展示甚至应用程序层也可以商品化的好方法。

> Kimi Work is a Local Agent designed for deep workflows. It mounts your local folders, navigates the web autonomously via WebBridge, runs Python code in the background, and executes scheduled tasks.

It's clearly a dupe of Claude/Codex products (Codex especially, styling-wise), but I think Kimi's goal here is simply to appear on feature-parity with bigger labs. I doubt they'd want to invest much into designing a UI for "the future of agentic work" from the ground up.

Their bread and butter and claim-to-fame will continue to be low-cost, near-frontier models, for which they need to at least appear to have some app layer to plug into.

And, in some ways, duping is a great way to show people that even the app layer can be commoditized.

楼层
zkmon 2026/07/21 12:24
我认为我们需要停止使用“欺骗”这个词，就好像某个地方有一些原创性不是建立在或复制已经存在的东西上的。
I think we need to stop using the term "dupe" as if there was some originality somewhere that was not built upon or copied what already existed.
楼层
QuantumGood 2026/07/21 20:15
“优秀的艺术家抄袭，伟大的艺术家偷窃，最好的艺术家让谎言显得真实。” – 史蒂夫·乔布斯。

事实上，乔布斯自私地重构了毕加索的这句话，

“优秀的艺术家复制，伟大的艺术家窃取。”
不过，乔布斯似乎确实正确地重新表达了毕加索的意图，正如毕加索所说：

“当有什么东西可以偷时，我就偷。”

  “艺术家临摹是什么意思？这很正常。重要的是做得更好。”

  “艺术是一系列巧妙的盗窃行为。”
"Good artists copy, great artists steal, the best artists make lies seem true." – Steve Jobs.

Actually, Jobs self-servingly refactored then misattributed to Picasso the line,

  "Good artists copy, great artists steal."
Jobs did seem to properly remarket Picasso's intent, though, as Picasso had said:

  "When there's anything to steal, I steal."

  "What does it mean, an artist copies? It is normal. The important thing is to do it better."

  "Art is a series of masterly thefts."
楼层
goolz 2026/07/21 23:59
最后一行太棒了！谢谢你这个人。老实说，我更喜欢原著，尽管它很啰嗦。
That last line is wonderful! Thank you for this man. I honestly like the original more despite it's wordiness.
楼层
all2 2026/07/22 00:40
这让我想起了约翰·威廉姆斯 (John Williams)，他是一位享有盛誉的艺术家，以《夺宝奇兵》OST 和《星球大战》OST 等作品而闻名。

他的所有作品（或大部分）都是对其他经典作品的大块内容进行了重新利用和改装。最美妙的是，他做得很好。

This makes me think of John Williams, an artist of great renown known for things like the Raiders of the Lost Ark OST and the Star Wars OSTs.

All of his stuff (or most of it) is repurposed and refitted chunks of other classical works. The beautiful part is, he does it so well.

楼层
geniium 2026/07/21 23:25
谢谢你让我微笑

我喜欢这句话，并且喜欢经常与周围的人分享

Thanks for making me smile

I love that quote and like to share it often with people around me

QuantumGood 2026/07/22 00:04
挺喜欢这种AI回复格式的，对你这条以及你所有的其他评论都存在明显的误解；这让识别像你这样的AI机器人变得容易多了。
Love the AI format with obvious misunderstanding to this and all your other comments; makes it much easier to find AI bots like yourself.

nonethewiser 2026/07/22 00:17
请解释前沿模型的复制来源。法学硕士一直存在吗？
Please explain where frontier models were copied from. Have LLMs always existed?
楼层
marginalia_nu 2026/07/21 23:12
获得灵感是一回事，这只是一个山寨货。 视觉语言几乎相同。 区别在于您是在其他东西的基础上构建，还是只是批量复制它而不做出任何贡献。
Taking inspiration is one thing, this is just a knock-off. The visual language is nearly identical. The difference is in whether you are building upon something else, or just copying it wholesale and contributing nothing of your own.

sdiepend 2026/07/21 16:52
“一切都是混音”https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xRHRUNpWzAY
"Everything is a Remix" https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xRHRUNpWzAY
楼层
cadamsdotcom 2026/07/21 10:49
有些范例完全符合人体工程学。有时人们只会接受一种用户体验。

没有人会购买一辆用踏板驾驶的汽车；没有人会购买一辆带有踏板的汽车。即使那更好（嘿，飞机就可以做到！） - 每个汽车制造商都在“复制”方向盘并将其安装在自己的汽车上。有争议吗？如果不是为什么不呢？

Some paradigms are simply ergonomic. Sometimes there's only one UX people will accept.

No one would buy a car steered with pedals; even if that is better (hey planes do it!) - every car maker is "copying" steering wheels and putting them on their cars. Is it controversial? If not why not?

楼层
mh- 2026/07/21 22:48
这并没有引起争议，因为帆船的现有技术已有大约 200 年的历史。

（在有方向盘之前，船舶是用舵柄来操纵的 - 早期的汽车也尝试过这种方式，从界面角度来看。）

It wasn't controversial because there was about 200 years of prior art from sailing ships.

(Before the wheel, ships were steered with tillers - which early cars also tried, interface-wise.)

楼层
thomasahle 2026/07/21 06:09
> 欺骗是向人们展示甚至应用程序层也可以商品化的好方法

每个人都已经在克隆应用程序层。有 30 多个 claude-code 克隆，并且 codex work 已经克隆了 cowork，grok 和 gemini 也在做同样的事情。

> duping is a great way to show people that even the app layer can be commoditized

Everyone is already cloning the app layer. There are 30+ claude-code clone, and codex work already cloned cowork, grok and gemini is doing the same.

楼层
CookieCrisp 2026/07/21 06:58
我强烈推荐那些经常使用它们的人 - 尝试克隆你自己的。我已经完全按照我想要的方式制作了我的产品，具有我在其他任何地方都没有见过的功能，并且集成到我使用的所有内容中。我们生活在一个每个应用程序都必须适合足够多的人的世界 - 但现在我们生活在一个应用程序可以被塑造成绝对适合您的世界！
I highly recommend to anyone that uses them a lot - try to clone your own. I’ve got mine fully formed into how I want it, with features I haven’t seen anywhere else yet and with integration into everything I work with. We’ve lived in a world where every app had to fit enough people well enough - but now we live in a world where an app can be molded to be absolutely perfect for just you!
楼层
Gigachad 2026/07/21 15:32
大多数人不想扮演产品经理，只是想花钱请人为他们做决定。

我们曾经有一段时间，每个小企业都有自己的定制会计软件，然后发现这些企业实际上并不是特殊的雪花，最好只使用通用软件并遵守它所做的任何事情

Most people don’t want to play product manager and just want to pay someone to make the decisions for them.

We used to have a time where every small business had its own custom accounting software, and discovered that these businesses aren’t actually special snowflakes and are better off just using the generic software and conforming to whatever it does

楼层
smallmancontrov 2026/07/22 00:42
他们之前的处境更好，因为生搬硬套现有方案的痛苦要小于自研软件的痛苦。但后者已经发生了根本性的变化，所以这个结论未必还成立。软件工程师最好至少开展一个副业项目，以此来校准自己对交付和维护全新项目成本的直觉，从而能够敏锐地发现那些初露端倪的利基市场，即便其他人还在靠借来的时间苟延残喘。
They were better off because the discomfort of shoehorning was less than the discomfort of in-house software development. The latter has changed radically so the conclusion may no longer hold. SWEs would do well to have at least one side project to calibrate their intuition about the cost of delivering and maintaining a greenfield project so that they can spot the niches which have begun to appear even as others live on borrowed time.
楼层
cdrini 2026/07/21 13:20
出于好奇，您最喜欢的、您的实现所独有的功能是什么？
Out of curiosity, what are your favourite features that are unique to your implementation?
楼层
embedding-shape 2026/07/21 16:06
我不是回复父评论，但我自己搭建的这套工具里最喜欢的一个功能，也是我在别处没见过的，就是能让智能体以透明且便捷的方式在不同主机上执行代码。实现起来相当简单，大概可以通过提示智能体加SSH配置来实现，不过当时我正在做其他与服务器<>客户端模型相关的工作，就想着干脆把它封装成几个工具加进去。现在，智能体可以构建跨平台应用，而我主要用Linux系统，却能直接验证应用是否正常运行，完全不需要我手动在三个操作系统之间来回切换。
Not parent, but favorite feature of my own harness I haven't seen somewhere else, is the ability for the agent to execute code on different hosts in a transparent and easy way. Fairly simple and probably could prompt the agent + set things up with ssh, but was doing other stuff related to a server<>client model, so figured why not add it as a couple of tools. Now the agent can build cross-platform applications while I'm mostly on Linux and actually verify it works, without me manually switching between three OSes.
楼层
mosselman 2026/07/21 16:34
当我让Claude，或者用Kimi、Codex打开代码来修复树莓派上的问题时，它们都会直接通过SSH连上去。

我发现，在各种AI工具包和工具中，大多数与工具包自身UI和UX无关的所谓“功能”，其实都可以通过技能或简单的提示词来实现。

Claude有一个很棒的功能，就是通过手机App进行远程控制。这可就不只是个简单的技能了。

When I tell Claude or open code using Kimi or codex to fix something on my raspberry pi, they all go to ssh right away.

I found that most things that are “features” in harnesses and tools that don’t relate to UI and UX in those harnesses themselves can be done through skills or just prompts.

A nice features of Claude’s is the remote control through the mobile app. That isn’t just a skill.

楼层
theplumber 2026/07/21 07:17
就是这样。你猜我现在在做什么工作？我自己的“工作”应用程序将比当今可用的任何其他应用程序都要好！
This is the way. Guess what am I working right now? My own “Work” app that will be better than anything else available today!
